nodes.purpose: Convert one allowedFiles path into a normalized repo-relative path, or reject it when it is absolute or escapes the repository.
nodes.outputSchema: normalized repo-relative path string, or null when invalid
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Read one DAG node allowedFiles list and collect the src/nodes/<folder> names that the node claims ownership of.
nodes.outputSchema: Set of node folder names referenced by allowedFiles
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Check whether one DAG node only lists files that are valid for its kind, keeping pure nodes inside their node folder except specs/contracts references.
nodes.outputSchema: array of human-readable allowedFiles errors
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Visit one node during depth-first cycle detection and record a cycle error when traversal re-enters the active path.
nodes.inputSchema: node: DagNode and current traversal path stack
nodes.outputSchema: void; appends cycle errors to local traversal state
invariants: Deterministic nested helper that only mutates local traversal sets.

nodes.purpose: Detect dependency cycles across the DAG by walking every node dependency chain with depth-first traversal.
nodes.outputSchema: array of human-readable cycle errors
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Validate an in-memory openDAG object for schema shape, unique ids, dependency integrity, documentation quality, acyclicity, and allowed file boundaries.
nodes.outputSchema: { ok: boolean, errors: string[], dag?: Dag }
invariants: The function is pure and deterministic.
Valid output has ok true and no errors.
Invalid output has ok false and at least one human-readable error.
Every dependency must refer to an existing node and the graph must be acyclic.
Allowed files must remain inside one node folder for pure nodes, with approved shell/template/skill paths for non-pure nodes.

nodes.purpose: Fill one node scaffold template by replacing every node-name placeholder with the requested node id.
nodes.outputSchema: rendered template text
invariants: Deterministic string helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Plan the three files and rendered file contents needed to scaffold a new TypeScript/Zod DAG node, without writing to the filesystem.
nodes.inputSchema: { nodeName: string, contractTemplate: string, implementationTemplate: string, testTemplate: string }
nodes.outputSchema: { files: planned file writes[], errors: string[] }
invariants: The function is pure and deterministic.
Valid node names produce exactly three planned files.
Invalid node names produce errors and no planned files.
Rendered files contain the requested node name wherever the template placeholder appears.

nodes.purpose: Plan the exact commands needed to verify one existing DAG node by checking that the node exists and has a colocated test file.
nodes.inputSchema: { dag: Dag, nodeName: string, existingFiles: string[] }
nodes.outputSchema: { commands: command plans[], errors: string[] }
invariants: The function is pure and deterministic.
A missing node produces errors and no commands.
A node without existing test files produces errors and no commands.
A valid node produces test and typecheck commands without executing them.

nodes.purpose: Calculate the vertical depth for one visual node so command entrypoints appear above the internal nodes they call.
nodes.inputSchema: node: DagNode, byId: Map<string, DagNode>, dependents: Map<string, string[]>, memo: Map<string, number>
nodes.outputSchema: depth number where 0 is an entrypoint row
invariants: Deterministic helper with only local memoization.

nodes.purpose: Assign one DAG node to a visual route so pure core, shell, support, generated, and skill nodes can be styled differently.
nodes.outputSchema: VisualRoute: main | shell | support | generated | skill
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Score one DAG node importance from its kind and number of dependents so major feature nodes render larger than helpers.
nodes.outputSchema: importance integer from 1 to 5
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Convert an importance score into fixed node card dimensions for deterministic graph layout.
nodes.outputSchema: { width: number, height: number }
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Choose the visual route style for one dependency edge based on the routes of its source and target nodes.
nodes.outputSchema: VisualRoute for the edge
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Decide whether one node should be visible in the default compact overview based on command status or high importance.
nodes.outputSchema: boolean indicating overview visibility
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Assign a stable lane priority to one overview node so the visualise feature route appears before secondary commands.
nodes.outputSchema: numeric lane priority where lower values render earlier
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Calculate compact overview depth for one visible node using only visible overview dependencies and command entrypoint anchors.
nodes.inputSchema: nodeId: string, visibleDependents: Map<string, string[]>, memo: Map<string, number>
nodes.outputSchema: overview depth number where 0 is a command row
invariants: Deterministic helper with only local memoization.

nodes.purpose: Transform a validated DAG into deterministic visual nodes, visual edges, route labels, importance scores, and full/overview coordinates for the interactive graph.
nodes.outputSchema: { nodes: VisualNode[], edges: VisualEdge[], counts, dimensions }
invariants: The function is pure and deterministic.
Every DAG node appears exactly once in the visual model.
Every dependency is represented as one visual edge from dependent to dependency.
Node depths place entrypoints above their internal dependencies.
Layout dimensions keep node cards inside the viewBox.
Overview coordinates provide a compact default layout for command and pure feature nodes.
Node kind is preserved for visual styling.
Pure core route nodes are visually more important than shell, support, generated, and skill routes.

nodes.purpose: Escape one text string for safe insertion into HTML element text or attribute contexts.
nodes.outputSchema: HTML-escaped string
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem or browser access.

nodes.purpose: Serialize one JSON value for embedding inside the generated HTML script tag without raw less-than characters.
nodes.outputSchema: safe JSON string for script payload embedding
invariants: Deterministic helper that escapes raw less-than characters.

nodes.purpose: Render a visual DAG model into a standalone interactive HTML document with search, route filters, overview/full modes, selected-node dependency expansion, details panel, and styled node cards.
nodes.outputSchema: { html: complete standalone HTML document string }
invariants: The function is pure and deterministic.
The output is a complete HTML document.
The output embeds the model data without raw less-than characters inside the JSON script payload.
The rendered page includes interactive search, overview/full mode, route filtering, status filtering, and node selection controls.
Node labels are clipped inside fixed-size cards instead of overflowing.
Non-pure node kinds have distinct visual styling.
Route and importance metadata are visible through labels, edge styles, and node size.
Selecting a node in overview mode reveals its hidden dependency chain without switching to the full graph.

nodes.purpose: Escape one browser runtime value before inserting it into generated visualisation DOM strings.
nodes.outputSchema: escaped display string
invariants: Template function is deterministic in the generated browser runtime.

nodes.purpose: Look up one visual node from the embedded browser model by its DAG node id.
nodes.outputSchema: VisualNode when found, otherwise undefined
invariants: Template function is deterministic for a fixed embedded model.

nodes.purpose: Build CSS variable declarations for one node status, kind, and route so the generated card receives the correct colours.
nodes.outputSchema: CSS variable declaration string
invariants: Template function is deterministic for a fixed node.

nodes.purpose: Render the five-step importance meter markup for one visual node in the details panel.
nodes.outputSchema: HTML string for the importance meter
invariants: Template function is deterministic for a fixed node.

nodes.purpose: Decide whether one node should currently be displayed after applying filters, overview/full mode, current selection, and selected-node dependency expansion.
nodes.inputSchema: node: VisualNode plus current DOM filter, view mode, and selection state
nodes.outputSchema: boolean indicating whether to show the node
invariants: Template function is deterministic for fixed DOM control values and node data.

nodes.purpose: Render one titled details-panel list, including an explicit None state when the list has no items.
nodes.outputSchema: HTML string for one details section
invariants: Template function is deterministic for fixed inputs.

nodes.purpose: Render the selected node details panel with route, kind, status, input, output, dependencies, dependents, invariants, and allowed files.
nodes.outputSchema: void; replaces the generated details panel DOM
invariants: Template function only mutates the generated details panel.

nodes.purpose: Determine whether one edge is connected to the currently selected node so it can be highlighted.
nodes.inputSchema: edge dataset with from and to node ids plus selectedId state
nodes.outputSchema: boolean indicating selected-edge connection
invariants: Template function is deterministic for selectedId and edge data.

nodes.purpose: Apply current dimensions, positions, edge paths, visibility, selection, and highlight CSS classes to the rendered SVG graph.
nodes.inputSchema: current generated graph DOM and filter, view mode, position, and selection state
nodes.outputSchema: void; mutates SVG attributes and CSS classes in the generated visualisation only
invariants: Template function only mutates generated graph CSS classes.

nodes.purpose: Set the selected node id, render details, and refresh graph visibility so hidden dependencies appear in a clickable top focus band.
nodes.inputSchema: id: string for the clicked visual node
nodes.outputSchema: void; updates generated visualisation selection and focus-band state
invariants: Template function only mutates generated visualisation selection state.

nodes.purpose: Select the correct coordinates for one node from the top focus band, shifted compact overview layout, or full graph layout.
nodes.inputSchema: node: VisualNode plus current view mode and expansion state
nodes.outputSchema: { x: number, y: number }
invariants: Template function is deterministic for the current view mode and node coordinates.

nodes.purpose: Compute the SVG cubic Bezier path for one dependency edge between two positioned visual nodes.
nodes.outputSchema: SVG path data string
invariants: Template function is deterministic for fixed model dimensions and node positions.

nodes.purpose: Create one interactive SVG group containing the node card foreignObject plus direct card and keyboard selection handlers.
nodes.outputSchema: SVGGElement for the rendered node
invariants: Template function creates DOM for the generated visualisation only.
Template function attaches click handling directly to generated cards so dynamically revealed nodes remain selectable.

nodes.purpose: Render the full SVG graph for the current view mode by drawing markers, edges, nodes, and initial visibility state.
nodes.inputSchema: embedded visualisation model and current view mode
nodes.outputSchema: void; replaces the generated SVG graph DOM
invariants: Template function only mutates the generated SVG graph.

nodes.purpose: Normalize user-provided text by trimming outer whitespace and replacing every internal whitespace run with one plain space.
nodes.outputSchema: { normalized: string }
invariants: The function is pure and deterministic.
The output normalized string has no leading or trailing whitespace.
Every non-empty whitespace run in the input becomes a single space in the output.
Normal words are preserved in their original order.

nodes.purpose: Read specs/dag.json or an explicit DAG path from disk and parse it as JSON for the validation shell.
nodes.outputSchema: parsed JSON value from the DAG file
invariants: Imperative shell function may read the filesystem.

nodes.purpose: Bridge filesystem DAG loading into the pure validateDag node and return the validation result to CLI callers.
nodes.outputSchema: ValidationResult with ok, errors, and parsed DAG when valid
invariants: Imperative shell boundary delegates validation to the pure DAG node.

nodes.purpose: Run the validate:dag CLI command by validating the repo DAG, printing errors or success, and setting process exit status.
nodes.inputSchema: process argv for npm run validate:dag
nodes.outputSchema: process output and exit code
invariants: Imperative shell function may write console output and exit the process.

nodes.purpose: Print create-node command usage and terminate the process when the caller did not provide a node name.
nodes.outputSchema: never; exits the process after console output
invariants: Imperative shell helper may write console output and exit the process.

nodes.purpose: Read one scaffold template file from the templates directory for the create-node shell.
nodes.outputSchema: template file contents string
invariants: Imperative shell helper may read template files.

nodes.purpose: Print verify-node command usage and terminate the process when the caller did not provide a node name.
nodes.outputSchema: never; exits the process after console output
invariants: Imperative shell helper may write console output and exit the process.

nodes.purpose: Run one subprocess command needed for node verification with inherited stdio from the current terminal.
nodes.outputSchema: void when the subprocess succeeds, otherwise throws/exits
invariants: Imperative shell helper may spawn child processes.

nodes.purpose: Run one full-project verification subprocess with inherited stdio from the current terminal.
nodes.outputSchema: void when the subprocess succeeds, otherwise throws/exits
invariants: Imperative shell helper may spawn child processes.

nodes.purpose: Open the generated DAG visualisation HTML file through the operating system default browser command.
nodes.outputSchema: void after dispatching the OS open command
invariants: Imperative shell helper may spawn OS file-opening commands.

nodes.purpose: Expose npm run validate:dag as the command entrypoint that validates specs/dag.json and reports contract violations.
nodes.inputSchema: process argv from npm run validate:dag
nodes.outputSchema: terminal validation report and process exit code
invariants: Top-level command entrypoint may perform process IO through shell functions only.

nodes.purpose: Expose npm run create:node as the command entrypoint that plans and writes a new node contract, implementation, and test scaffold.
nodes.outputSchema: created node scaffold files or terminal errors
invariants: Top-level command entrypoint may write files only through the shell after pure scaffold planning.

nodes.purpose: Expose npm run verify:node as the command entrypoint that validates the DAG and runs tests/typecheck for one named node.
nodes.outputSchema: terminal verification report and process exit code
invariants: Top-level command entrypoint may read files and run verification subprocesses through shell functions only.

nodes.purpose: Expose npm run verify:all as the command entrypoint that validates the DAG, checks generated docs, runs all tests, and typechecks the repo.
nodes.inputSchema: process argv from npm run verify:all
nodes.outputSchema: full-project verification report and process exit code
invariants: Top-level command entrypoint may run full-project verification through shell functions only.

nodes.purpose: Expose npm run visualise as the command entrypoint that validates the DAG, builds the visual model, renders HTML, writes it to disk, and optionally opens it.
nodes.outputSchema: generated HTML file path and optional browser open side effect
invariants: Top-level command entrypoint may write/open files only after pure visual model and HTML rendering.

nodes.purpose: Walk one node inside the skill bundled DAG checker script and report cycles in a lightweight skill-installation context.
nodes.inputSchema: node id and active traversal stack
nodes.outputSchema: void; mutates local script traversal state
invariants: Skill helper mutates only local script traversal state.

nodes.purpose: Walk a repository directory for the converter skill while ignoring dependency, build, cache, and VCS folders.
nodes.outputSchema: string[] of discovered repo-relative paths
invariants: Skill helper is intentionally imperative because it scans the filesystem.

nodes.purpose: Define the placeholder pure run function shape used when create-node generates a new node implementation file.
nodes.inputSchema: Input type from the generated node contract
nodes.outputSchema: Output type from the generated node contract
invariants: Template function describes the contract-first node shape.

nodes.purpose: Define the placeholder TypeScript/Zod run function shape distributed inside the reusable functional DAG agent skill.
nodes.inputSchema: Input type from the skill node contract template
nodes.outputSchema: Output type from the skill node contract template
invariants: Template function describes the reusable contract-first node shape.

nodes.purpose: Package reusable Codex instructions, templates, and helper scripts for applying the functional DAG workflow while implementing coding tasks.
nodes.inputSchema: Codex task context for a repo that may or may not already use openDAG
nodes.outputSchema: workflow guidance, templates, and helper scripts for DAG-based implementation
invariants: Skill artifact is tracked in the DAG as a reusable workflow component.

nodes.purpose: Package reusable Codex instructions, templates, and helper scripts for converting an existing repository toward contract-first functional DAG architecture.
nodes.inputSchema: existing repository source tree and conversion goal
nodes.outputSchema: conversion workflow, starter DAG template, AGENTS template, and repo scan helper
invariants: Skill artifact is tracked in the DAG as a reusable conversion component.

nodes.purpose: Check one DAG node documentation fields for placeholder text and a clear enough purpose before validation succeeds.
nodes.outputSchema: array of human-readable documentation errors
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Escape one text value so generated Markdown table/list content cannot break pipe-delimited formatting.
nodes.outputSchema: Markdown-safe string
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Render one string list for the node catalog, using inline code entries or an explicit None marker for empty lists.
nodes.outputSchema: Markdown inline list string
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Render one DAG node into a Markdown section stating what it does, input it expects, output it gives, dependencies, invariants, and allowed files.
nodes.outputSchema: Markdown section string
invariants: Deterministic helper with no filesystem access.

nodes.purpose: Generate the complete Markdown node catalog from the DAG so every node has maintained documentation for behavior, expected input, and produced output.
nodes.outputSchema: { markdown: string }
invariants: The function is pure and deterministic.
Every DAG node appears exactly once in the generated catalog.
Every node section states what the node does, the input it expects, and the output it gives.
Node sections are sorted by node id for stable diffs.

nodes.purpose: Resolve the repository output path for the generated node catalog markdown file.
nodes.outputSchema: absolute path to specs/node_catalog.md
invariants: Imperative shell helper only reads process cwd.

nodes.purpose: Validate the DAG through the shell loader and build the generated node catalog markdown from the parsed DAG.
nodes.outputSchema: generated Markdown catalog string
invariants: Imperative shell helper reads DAG only through the existing validation boundary.

nodes.purpose: Compare the expected generated catalog with the existing file on disk and report whether they are identical.
nodes.inputSchema: expected markdown string and outputPath string
nodes.outputSchema: boolean freshness result
invariants: Imperative shell helper only reads the generated catalog file.

nodes.purpose: Run the generate-node-docs CLI by generating catalog markdown, checking freshness with --check, or writing specs/node_catalog.md.
nodes.outputSchema: terminal report, generated file write, and process exit code
invariants: Imperative shell helper writes only the generated node catalog file.

nodes.purpose: Expose npm run generate:docs as the command entrypoint that regenerates specs/node_catalog.md from specs/dag.json.
nodes.inputSchema: process argv from npm run generate:docs
nodes.outputSchema: updated specs/node_catalog.md and terminal report
invariants: Top-level command entrypoint may write only generated documentation after DAG validation succeeds.

nodes.purpose: Expose npm run check:docs as the command entrypoint that fails when specs/node_catalog.md is stale relative to specs/dag.json.
nodes.inputSchema: process argv from npm run check:docs
nodes.outputSchema: freshness report and process exit code
invariants: Top-level command entrypoint only compares generated documentation and does not mutate files.

nodes.purpose: Expose npm run build as the package build entrypoint that compiles TypeScript source and CLI tools into dist for npm publication.
nodes.inputSchema: process argv from npm run build
nodes.outputSchema: compiled dist artifacts and process exit code
invariants: Top-level package build entrypoint may invoke the TypeScript compiler through the npm shell only.
Build output is generated from committed source, contracts, and tool entrypoints.

nodes.purpose: Represent npm pack and npm publish package assembly, including docs, skills, templates, specs, compiled dist files, and package metadata.
nodes.inputSchema: npm pack or npm publish process context
nodes.outputSchema: package tarball contents or npm registry publication result
invariants: Package assembly must run verification and build through npm lifecycle scripts before publication.
Published artifacts must come from declared package files and generated dist output.

nodes.purpose: Collect the transitive dependency ids for one selected visual node in the generated browser graph.
nodes.inputSchema: id: string and optional visited Set<string>
nodes.outputSchema: Set<string> of dependency node ids reachable from the selected node
invariants: Template function is deterministic for a fixed embedded model and selected node id.

nodes.purpose: Return the dependency ids that should be force-visible for the currently selected node in overview mode.
nodes.inputSchema: selectedId state and current view mode
nodes.outputSchema: Set<string> of dependency ids to reveal in overview mode
invariants: Template function returns an empty set outside overview mode or when no node is selected.

nodes.purpose: Decide whether one visual node belongs to the default overview before any selected-node dependency expansion.
nodes.outputSchema: boolean indicating default overview visibility
invariants: Template function is deterministic for a fixed visual node.

nodes.purpose: Build the sorted local focus list containing the selected hidden node and its hidden dependencies that are not already visible in compact overview.
nodes.inputSchema: selectedId state, current view mode, and embedded visualisation model
nodes.outputSchema: VisualNode[] for the local focus expansion layout
invariants: Template function keeps the selected hidden node first and sorts remaining dependency nodes by id for stable placement.

nodes.purpose: Assign one selected hidden node or force-visible hidden dependency a stable position inside the top focus band.
nodes.inputSchema: node: VisualNode plus selected-node dependency expansion state
nodes.outputSchema: { x: number, y: number } when part of the focus band, otherwise null
invariants: Template function places focus-band nodes in stable rows above the shifted overview graph.

nodes.purpose: Compute the SVG dimensions for the current view mode, reserving top space for local selected-node dependency expansion.
nodes.inputSchema: current view mode, embedded model dimensions, and selected dependency expansion state
nodes.outputSchema: { width: number, height: number } for the SVG viewBox
invariants: Template function keeps focus-band dependency nodes and shifted overview nodes inside the generated SVG viewBox.

nodes.purpose: Compute the reserved top focus-band height needed to show selected hidden nodes and dependencies without overlapping the overview graph.
nodes.inputSchema: selected-node expansion list in overview mode
nodes.outputSchema: height in SVG units to reserve above the overview graph
invariants: Template function returns zero when no hidden dependency focus band is needed.
